passage: 莊子釣於濮水，楚王使大夫二人往先焉，曰：「願以境內累矣！」莊子持竿不顧，曰：「吾聞楚有神龜，死已三千歲矣，王巾笥而藏之廟堂之上。此龜者，寧其死為留骨而貴乎？寧其生而曳尾於塗中乎？」二大夫曰：「寧生而曳尾塗中。」莊子曰：「往矣！吾將曳尾於塗中。」

之.english: zhi — it, him, her; possessive/genitive marker

以.english: yi — with, by means of, in order to

children.english: fu — adult man; also a title (officer)

夫.english: fu — adult man; also a title (officer)

子.english: zi — master, philosopher; also child, seed

巾.english: jin — cloth, kerchief; here "wrapped in silk"

曳.english: ye — to drag, trail, pull along

楚.english: Chu — ancient state south of the Yellow River; also "distinct, painful"

濮.english: Pu — name of a river in Henan

children.english: fu — adult man; also a title (officer)

為.english: wei — for, as, to be, do

留.english: liu — to keep, remain, leave behind

矣.english: yi — classical sentence-final particle (change of state)

累.english: lei — to burden, entangle; also "pile up"

莊.english: Zhuang — surname of Master Zhuang (Zhuangzi); also "solemn, dignified"

children.english: fu — adult man; also a title (officer)

釣.english: diao — to fish with hook and line

顧.english: gu — to look back, regard, attend to
